items.title: Why isn't One Nation winning many seats in the SA election despite its high vote?

children.content: One Nation has received more than 20 per cent of the primary vote in South Australia, and so far, its lower house seat count remains slight. Why is that, and why are onlookers so interested in the result?

gallery.altText: A person voting on a sample House of Representatives ballot paper, with names like Apple and Peach

thumbnail.altText: A person voting on a sample House of Representatives ballot paper, with names like Apple and Peach

items.title: Geelong downs Adelaide in wet-weather thriller as hail hits ground

children.content: Geelong continues its streak over Adelaide at Kardinia Park, surviving a spirited fightback from the Crows to claim an eight-point win to kick off Round 3. Follow live.

image.altText: Tom Stewart tackles Ben Keays

items.title: Premier says regional SA will be first in line for fuel amid global crisis

children.content: South Australia's premier has assured farmers battling with soaring diesel prices that regional areas will be first to receive fuel deliveries.

image.altText: The silhouette of a man wearing a trucker cap standing next to a petrol bowser with a pump in hand.

items.title: 'My left foot was in his mouth': Fisher's lucky escape from 3.5m great white shark

children.content: A fisher known as "Lucky Luke" to his mates lived up to his nickname after surviving a great white shark dragging him along the ocean floor while lobster fishing off the South Australian coastline.

gallery.altText: A man a black and red t-shirt holds two rock lobsters in front of a boat

thumbnail.altText: A man a black and red t-shirt holds two rock lobsters in front of a boat

items.title: Ryswyk says reaction to coming out has been 'overwhelming'

children.content: Former Brisbane Lions player Leigh Ryswyk says the support he received after becoming the first AFL player to come out as gay has "blown me away".

image.altText: Leigh Ryswyk, North Adelaide Football Club, about to kick a ball in an Aussie rules match

items.title: Rain wrecks day one of Shield final after South Australia's top order buckles

children.content: Victoria sent South Australia in to bat on day one of the Sheffield Shield final and claimed three wickets before rain stopped play.

image.altText: Alex Carey prepares to bat for South Australia in the Sheffield Shield final

items.title: Man dubbed 'miracle baby' when he was born dies aged 33

children.content: Ryan Bowman fought through congenital heart defects and cancer, but his family says his final hospital visit led to some frightening final hours.

gallery.altText: A man and woman holding a photo of their son.

items.title: Chris Picton replaced as health minister in SA cabinet reshuffle

children.content: South Australian Premier Peter Malinauskas has unveiled his new-look second-term cabinet. Among the changes, Chris Picton has been moved out of the health portfolio and replaced by former education minister Blair Boyer.

image.altText: A woman shakes a man's hand and looks towards a crowd.

items.title: Why SA farmers are turning from the Liberal Party towards One Nation

children.content: Rural SA electorates that were once safe Liberal seats are now marginal, thanks to voters swinging towards One Nation.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson meets with a fan.

items.title: 'I demand discipline': Why Labor's second term could be harder than its first

children.content: Despite holding an overwhelming majority in parliament, the Malinauskas government faces no shortage of policy and political challenges to navigate in its second term.

gallery.altText: Peter Malinauskas shaking hands with Deputy Premier Kyam Maher

items.title: Three people killed, two injured in crash involving car and truck

children.content: The sedan driver had life-threatening injuries and a fourth passenger was seriously injured.

image.altText: The scene of an Adelaide Hills crash involving a truck and a car.

items.title: Title beckons as South Australia peak for Sheffield Shield final

children.content: Victory in this year's Sheffield Shield final over Victoria will be especially sweet for South Australia, with coach Ryan Harris saying their path to the title decider this time around has been challenging.

image.altText: Nathan McAndrew of South Australia running through after bowling the cricket ball

items.title: The One Nation vote in SA

children.content: One Nation just sent a shiver through the Liberal Party, after a breakthrough result in South Australia.
Pauline Hanson's party achieved more than 21 per cent of the statewide primary vote, beating the Liberal Party and becoming the second-highest polling party.
But nationally, more attention has been on the undercard
fight, and that has left the Liberal Party deeply wounded.

image.altText: TN_OneNation_Loop

items.title: Major facelift proposed for Mount Gambier's Umpherston Sinkhole garden

children.content: The Mount Gambier council is asking for feedback on a significant change to the regional city's beloved sunken garden.

image.altText: A large hole in the ground filled with flowers and vines and palm trees

items.title: 'Blue blob' vandal who gave sculpture googly eyes ordered to pay compensation

children.content: A 20-year-old woman who made headlines by putting googly eyes on a controversial sculpture in the middle of Mount Gambier is ordered to pay $2,000 to the city's council.

image.altText: A large blue sculpture with gold tape and grey markings on it in front of an orange fence.

items.title: Remote town cut off by rain gets 'weird phone call' with anonymous donation

children.content: An anonymous donor funded two planes full of supplies for the outback town of Marree, near Kati Thanda-Lake Eyre, after it was cut off by flooding.

items.title: Labor caucus chooses new cabinet ministers as Liberals pledge election review

children.content: As SA Liberal leader Ashton Hurn pledged to review the weekend's catastrophic defeat for her party, the Labor caucus met for the first time since its resounding win, with the premier hitting back at remarks made by One Nation's Pauline Hanson.

image.altText: A composite image of Ashton Hurn and Peter Malinauskas.

items.title: Southern Hemisphere's longest-running abalone farm destocking ahead of closure

children.content: Fifteen permanent jobs will be lost on Kangaroo Island with the closure of Yumbah Aquaculture's abalone farm.

image.altText: Close up of blue-grey shells of abalone in water.

items.title: Lift minimum wage above the rate of inflation, federal government urges

children.content: In its submission to the Fair Work Commission's annual wage review, the government again calls for the lowest-paid workers to not go backwards.

image.altText: A woman speaks to the media.

items.title: Fuel costs drive COVID-like surge in demand for emergency relief

children.content: Rising petrol and diesel prices are driving Canberra families to seek emergency assistance and forcing small businesses to consider whether to pass on higher costs to customers.

image.altText: A Vinnie's worker from behind.

items.title: Drug-fuelled driver who hit schoolboys sues Commonwealth over arrest

children.content: Tayler Hazell, who will be sentenced tomorrow over a hit-and-run that seriously injured two children, is suing the federal government, alleging police violently treated him during his arrest.

image.altText: Bodycam arret Tayler Hazell

items.title: Man who allegedly lured actors into sex acts handed 45 more charges

children.content: Police have laid 45 more charges dating back to 2014 against a Sydney man accused of posing as a Disney casting agent and offering movie opportunities for sex acts, with a magistrate describing it as "some of the most manipulative behaviour I've seen".

image.altText: a man is taken outof a police van after being arrested forposing as an agent to allegely lure people for sex

items.title: Here's who you should see at comedy festivals across Australia

children.content: Comedy season is in full swing, with festivals across the country hosting the best acts from Australia and the world. We asked our favourites to share their recommendations.

items.title: What should you eat to keep your brain sharp as you get older?

children.content: The food choices we make over decades — not just in later life, but across adulthood — may quietly shape the health of our brains in ways that only become visible much later.

image.altText: A bowl of salad - cherry tomatoes, lettuce, sweet potato, capsicum and cabbage

items.title: Vegetable growers won't plant more crops if costs keep rising

children.content: A snap survey by AUSVEG reveals 27 per cent of Australian vegetable growers have already reduced planting, while some will not harvest current crops because rising costs make it unviable.

image.altText: Vegetable farmer Steve Kluck holds two lettuces in his hands.

items.title: TGA proposes removing sunscreen SPF numbers in regulation changes

children.content: The proposals include a number of changes to SPF testing requirements, oversight of the labs that do SPF testing, and the way SPF is labelled — reforms that would overhaul the sunscreen industry.

image.altText: bottle of sunscreen

items.title: Senior NT Police officer demoted 'because of race', lawsuit claims

children.content: A former NT Police Acting Assistant Commissioner has claimed his career is in tatters after he was overlooked for promotion and demoted for "reasons including" his race.

image.altText: A police officer in a police uniform speaks at a press conference

children.content: The Socceroos' upcoming games against Cameroon and Curaçao could make or shatter World Cup dreams, and Socceroos legend Mark Schwarzer has backed the players, and coach Tony Popovic, to deliver come tournament time.

items.title: 'Here I am': Sarah Mullally enthroned as first female Archbishop of Canterbury

children.content: Dame Sarah Mullally is enthroned as the Church of England's first female Archbishop of Canterbury, taking the helm as the spiritual leader of 85 million Anglicans worldwide in a historic ceremony blending tradition with global symbolism.

articles.title: Australian bishop surprised by appointment to senior Vatican role

children.content: Anthony Randazzo, the bishop of Broken Bay, which since 2019 covers Sydney's northern regions and the central coast, receives the personal title of archbishop and is named as Prefect of the Dicastery for Legislative Texts.
